title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers transactional outbox, relay, inbox, sagas, and compensation into a new outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.
Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 4962 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Dual Write Failure Matrix.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Dual Write Failure Matrix, keep Dual Write Failure Matrix connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Saga Compensation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Saga Compensation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Saga Compensation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab messaging team handles case 1010 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Saga Compensation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Saga Compensation without its stated preconditions, omit the outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Saga Compensation for outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Saga Compensation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Saga Compensation output and hide the violated outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Saga Compensation.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Saga Compensation
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Saga Compensation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab messaging team handles case 1915 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Saga Compensation.
steps.why: Retrieving Saga Compensation through the concrete outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Saga Compensation, keep Saga Compensation connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.
